name: Glycolysis (Embden-Meyerhof-Parnas)
description: Glycolysis converts glucose to two pyruvate through an energy-investment phase that spends ATP to make fructose-1,6-bisphosphate, cleavage to two triose phosphates, and a payoff phase that oxidizes glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate to generate NADH and substrate-level ATP, requiring NAD+ regeneration to continue.
mermaid:
graph TD
    N1["Glucose"]
    N2["Investment: phosphorylate"]
    N3["Fructose-1,6-bisP"]
    N4["Cleave to two trioses"]
    N5["G3P x2"]
    N6["Oxidation + NAD+ -> NADH"]
    N7["Payoff: substrate-level ATP"]
    N8{"Net 2 ATP + 2 NADH?"}
    N9["Regenerate NAD+"]
    N10["2 Pyruvate"]

    N1 --> N2
    N2 --> N3
    N3 --> N4
    N4 --> N5
    N5 --> N6
    N6 --> N7
    N7 --> N8
    N8 -->|need NAD+| N9
    N9 -->|recycle| N6
    N8 -->|yes| N10

    style N1 fill:#ff6b6b,color:#fff
    style N2 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N3 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N4 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N5 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N6 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N7 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N8 fill:#74c0fc,color:#fff
    style N9 fill:#51cf66,color:#fff
    style N10 fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
